hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (574) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,278, a 45% ratio), and overheal (73.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (59.5%) by 14.3 points - a real, below-average 36th-percentile kill, though Shlicktree still ranked 1st of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing on this kill. Zero real deaths logged.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's HoT tick ran 12.5 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (53.6% vs 41.1%), while Regrowth ran 8.1 points behind (19.5% vs 27.6%) - a real, moderate composition gap.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Neither Innervate nor Nature's Swiftness was cast this kill, against real 77%/84%-used Top 100 norms. No deaths to answer.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (85.9% vs 83.5% average) tracked close to the Top 100 norm, but top-target concentration (36.6% vs 48.5% average, -11.9) ran well below typical, across 10 distinct targets - healing spread more evenly than the benchmark usually shows here.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (903) ran below the Top 100 average (1,615, a 56% ratio), and overheal (55.7%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (49.1%) by 6.6 points - still a solid 70th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with 3 real deaths logged.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation ran 13.2 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (27.1% vs 13.9%) while Lifebloom's HoT tick ran 13.1 points behind (15.4% vs 28.5%) - a real inversion of the raid's usual Lifebloom-led pattern on this kill.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate and Nature's Swiftness both went uncast again (90%/75%-used norms). Three real deaths (Captinspanky, Vajony, Hatchpeppers) occurred with no Rebirth cast - the nearest logged cooldowns (Mana Potion, Swiftmend) land within 2.7-20.9 seconds of each death but were self-targeted or aimed elsewhere, not a real save attempt.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (65.8% vs 56.8% average, +9.0) and top-target concentration (21.5% vs 25.3% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's second-widest spread (20 distinct targets).

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (681) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,535, a 44% ratio), and overheal (62.2%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (54.3%) by 7.9 points - a middling 42nd-percentile kill (3rd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank, 4th by raw healing) on the raid's highest-death kill (8 real deaths).
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth fell 40.7 points below its Top 100 benchmark share (12.1% vs 52.8%) - the largest single-spell composition gap of the entire raid - while Rejuvenation ran 25.1 points ahead (38.4% vs 13.3%).
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate and Nature's Swiftness again went uncast (94%/81%-used norms). Rebirth was cast once, on Churbert, about 54 seconds after Churbert's own death; none of the other 7 real deaths this kill land close enough to any cooldown to call a real save - the nearby Swiftmend and Mana Potion casts are coincidental timing.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (50.5% vs 51.4% average) and top-target concentration (12.4% vs 15.6% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 20 distinct targets. Shlicktree's own name is the second-highest healing target (11.9%), consistent with the boss's heavy raid damage and high death count.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: The low point of the raid: a self-death just 34 seconds into the encounter cut active time to 28.1% (Top 100 average 99.2%) and produced a real 0th-percentile result - last of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing this kill. HPS (322, 17% of the Top 100 average) reflects the shortened window Shlicktree was alive to heal in as much as raw output.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth carried 30.1 points more than its Top 100 benchmark share (63.9% vs 33.8%) while Lifebloom's HoT tick ran 23.8 points behind (16.2% vs 40.0%) - a composition shaped by the short window Shlicktree was alive to heal in before the self-death.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shlicktree's own death (34 seconds into the fight) came about 11 seconds before Deepbowlz's, leaving no Rebirth available to answer either death or the third (Danceswtrees, roughly 2:42 into the fight, near the encounter's end). Innervate and Nature's Swiftness weren't cast, but with active time cut to 28.1% by the early death, the 69-80%-used Top 100 norms for those cooldowns carry less weight here than on a full-length kill.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (80.6% vs 74.6% average) and top-target concentration (35.2% vs 34.8% average) both tracked almost exactly on the Top 100 norm despite the shortened active window, across 8 distinct targets.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (778) ran below the Top 100 average (1,447, a 54% ratio), and overheal (64.1%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (51.1%) by 13.0 points - a middling 43rd-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with zero real deaths logged.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation ran 16.4 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (29.4% vs 13.0%); Lifebloom and Regrowth both tracked within 3.1 points of their benchmark shares.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate and Nature's Swiftness both went uncast against a real 93%/87%-used Top 100 norm - the highest Nature's Swiftness usage rate of any boss this raid, making this the widest statistical gap for that cooldown all night. No deaths to answer.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (89.7% vs 67.4% average, +22.3) ran the furthest above the Top 100 norm of any kill this raid, concentrated on the tank Turbarct (40.3% of total healing) across a narrower 9-target spread.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (682) ran below the Top 100 average (1,142, a 60% ratio, the closest HPS-to-benchmark ratio of the raid), and overheal (55.0%) sits just 1.5 points above the sample's single worst parse (53.5%) - a real but narrow gap - on a solid 57th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 1st by raw healing, with 5 real deaths logged on the raid's longest fight (498 seconds).
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's HoT tick ran 15.7 points behind its Top 100 benchmark share (31.0% vs 46.7%) while Regrowth ran 9.5 points ahead (31.4% vs 21.9%) - composition leaned toward direct healing rather than the benchmark's Lifebloom-heavy norm.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate was cast once, on Lippies, in line with the 98%-used norm; Nature's Swiftness again went uncast (86%-used norm). Rebirth was cast once, on Destromental, about 3 minutes after Destromental's own death - a real, slow response; the other 4 real deaths (Purehentai, Deepbowlz, Vinnyvoss, Captinspanky) were never rebirthed.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (54.2% vs 50.9% average) and top-target concentration (19.8% vs 21.0% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's widest spread (24 distinct targets).

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (425) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,080, a 39% ratio - the weakest HPS ratio of the raid), and overheal (73.5%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (61.1%) by 12.4 points - the raid's second-lowest percentile at 17th, ranking 3rd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 2nd by raw healing. Zero real deaths logged.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's HoT tick fell 21.1 points below its Top 100 benchmark share (28.8% vs 49.9%) - the second-largest composition gap of the raid - while Regrowth and Rejuvenation both ran ahead of benchmark (+9.6 and +12.1 points).
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate and Nature's Swiftness both went uncast again (92%/85%-used norms). No deaths to answer.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (86.7% vs 78.4% average, +8.3) ran above the Top 100 norm, concentrated on Jotes (45.6% of total healing, the raid's highest single-target concentration) across a narrower 11-target spread.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,154, the raid's highest raw HPS) still ran below the Top 100 average (2,465, a 47% ratio), and overheal (29.3%) came the closest of any kill this raid to the sample's single worst parse (33.0%, only 3.7 points shy) - a 28th-percentile kill, ranking 4th of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with 6 real deaths logged, the second-most of the raid.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth fell 50.0 points below its Top 100 benchmark share (26.4% vs 76.4%) - the largest single-spell composition gap of the entire raid - while Lifebloom's HoT tick ran 22.1 points ahead (27.0% vs 4.9%). Real Tranquility healing also shows up here (guid 44208, 4.1% of total) - see the cooldown note below for why the cooldown table still reads 0 casts for it.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate was cast once, on Lippies, in line with the 99%-used norm; Nature's Swiftness again went uncast (54%-used norm). Six real deaths (Banepain, Raezy, Churbert, Purehentai, Sackajaweeba, Héz) occurred with no Rebirth cast - Void Reaver's own Top 100 Rebirth usage rate is only 7%, so this isn't a statistical outlier, but it's still the raid's second-highest death count left unanswered. Tranquility shows 0 casts in this table despite real Tranquility healing in the spell-composition data above (guid 44208) - that's the known, documented gap where Tranquility's cooldown-tracking guid hasn't been added to this pipeline yet, not evidence the ability wasn't used.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (50.2% vs 37.2% average, +13.0) ran well above the Top 100 norm - the raid's second-largest positive coverage gap - across the second-widest spread (23 distinct targets).

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (547) ran below the Top 100 average (1,239, a 44% ratio), and overheal (73.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (65.4%) by 8.4 points - a solid 67th-percentile kill, ranking 3rd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with zero real deaths logged.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth fell 23.8 points below its Top 100 benchmark share (35.4% vs 59.2%) while Rejuvenation and Lifebloom's HoT tick both ran ahead (+14.1 and +12.0 points respectively).
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate was cast once, self-targeted this time (in line with the 82%-used norm, though only 41% of the Top 100 sample self-targets it here); Nature's Swiftness again went uncast (72%-used norm). No deaths to answer.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (57.9% vs 52.9% average) and top-target concentration (22.7% vs 16.2% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 19 distinct targets. Shlicktree's own name appears among the top 5 healing targets (7.8%).

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (586) ran below the Top 100 average (1,018, a 58% ratio) on the raid's longest kill (476 seconds), and overheal (62.3%) sits just 0.6 points shy of the sample's single worst parse (61.7%) - a middling 50th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 1st by raw healing, with 4 real deaths logged.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom's HoT tick fell 18.8 points below its Top 100 benchmark share (20.6% vs 39.4%) while Regrowth and Rejuvenation both ran ahead (+6.6 and +7.0 points). Real Tranquility healing shows up here too (guid 44208, 6.0% of total, the raid's largest Tranquility contribution) - see the cooldown note below for why the cooldown table still reads 0 casts.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: This is the raid's one real statistical outlier for Rebirth: 62% of the Top 100 sample casts it on Kael'thas - the highest Rebirth usage rate of any boss this raid, reflecting the fight's death-heavy phoenix/nether-vapor mechanics - yet Shlicktree never cast it despite 4 real deaths (Banepain, Sackajaweeba, Deepbowlz, Vinnyvoss). Nature's Swiftness again went uncast (87%-used norm). None of the 4 deaths land close enough to any cooldown to call a specific missed save. Tranquility again shows 0 casts here despite real healing in the composition table above (guid 44208) - the same known tracking gap noted on Void Reaver.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (51.9% vs 57.7% average) and top-target concentration (17.7% vs 20.8% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's widest spread (26 distinct targets) on its longest kill.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: Consistent across all 10 kills, aside from one real, ongoing variant: the chest piece (item 30216) keeps the same permanent enchant throughout, but a temporary enchant (id 2791) is present on Morogrim through Kael'thas (6 kills) and absent on the first four (Hydross, Lurker, Leotheras, Karathress) - flagged for review since it doesn't match the usual single-kill-swap pattern seen elsewhere in this pipeline.
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: 8 of 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant on every kill; the Back slot (Cape, item 279240) never does - the one persistent, real gear gap this raid night.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: All 10 real SSC/TK bosses attempted were killed this raid night. Shlicktree ranked 1st of 5 tracked healers present by item-level rank on 2 of 10 kills (41% average rank percentile) and 1st by raw healing on 4 of 10. Percentiles ranged widely, from 0th (Karathress) to 70th (Lurker), with Karathress, Al'ar (17th), and Void Reaver (28th) the raid's three lowest kills. Raw HPS trailed the Top 100 average on every single kill (17%-60% of the benchmark average), and overheal matched or exceeded the Top 100 sample's own single worst parse on 8 of 10 kills, with Void Reaver landing within 3.7 points of that bar on the other. Nature's Swiftness was never cast on any of the 10 kills, against Top 100 usage norms of 54-87% depending on the boss - the raid's single most consistent cooldown gap. Deaths concentrated on Leotheras (8) and Void Reaver (6); Karathress's own self-death just 34 seconds into that encounter, followed by two more deaths after Shlicktree was already down, was the raid's clear low point (0th percentile, 28.1% active time). Rebirth's most notable real gap was on Kael'thas, where 62% of the Top 100 sample uses it against 4 real deaths here and zero Rebirth casts.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Fathom-Lord Karathress landed at the 0th percentile after a self-death just 34 seconds into the fight left only 28.1% active time for the rest of the encounter, and Nature's Swiftness was never cast on any of the 10 kills this raid night.

GearCheckItems.Description: Elixir of Healing Power (Battle) and Elixir of Draenic Wisdom (Guardian) both active on all 10 kills, plus Well Fed food buff
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A complete consumable setup across the whole raid night - the Battle + Guardian Elixir pairing is a fully valid alternative to a Flask under the real TBC rule, and Flask itself was never active any kill.
